source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: A typical Hoonah [woman], Alaska, 1903
description: 1903 sepia photograph of a Huna Tlingit woman in a traditional dugout canoe, holding a paddle, on the calm water off Hoonah with village clan houses visible in the background. Published by LaRoche of Seattle. The original LoC catalog title uses a dated term now considered pejorative; the image itself documents Huna Tlingit canoe culture at the turn of the 20th century.
alt: 1903 sepia photograph of a Huna Tlingit woman in a traditional dugout canoe with a paddle, Hoonah village in the background
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-12 — downloaded from loc.gov storage-services (cph 3c27137) and visually confirmed
